checks.reason: All AC nodes use ac<N>-<verb>-<obj> prefix. Plumbing nodes use setup-/teardown-/gate-.
No switch-default AC bypass, no eval_sync skip-reason, no wait>500ms, no DOM-only visual claims, no missing screenshots for visual ACs.
ac1 uses eq on accepted field and price set check. ac2 uses lt operator comparing sl price to entry price.
Each AC has a screenshot node. Screenshots visually confirm +15 accepted in TP, -5 creates SL below entry.
Modal closed via teardown-close-modal after all ACs.
Recipe is small and focused: navigates to perps position, opens TPSL modal, asserts two ACs, closes modal.

ac1-assert-tp-value-accepted checks accepted:true — reverting isSignedDecimalInput fix causes this to fail. ac2-assert-sl-price-below-entry checks sl < entry — reverting percentToPrice fix causes this to fail.

better_version_guidance: Could also test SL preset button click produces a negative percent display, and that short positions invert correctly. Current recipe focuses on the primary user-facing bug.
